per_deal_summaries.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale; ~2K calls/week at 2 pilot locs (4 total; 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Jim (CEO); Deana Guy-Johnson (Clinical Manager, 10 yrs at GLOC)
per_deal_summaries.status: Signed April 27 — Solv's first Monarch design partner. Channel renamed #mon-partner-gloc. Kickoff doc live. Kennedy handling Maya setup (DID phone numbers, routing), Carli handling Athena 3rd-party API access and preview instance. Kapeesh requested a Drive folder and design partner onboarding SOP to document learnings. Jim's leadership team on vacation until ~May 16; formal external kickoff call pending.
key_risks: Phase 1 is $0 — margin begins only once resolution rate hits ~35%, unproven in ortho
Deana (Clinical Manager) was openly skeptical AI can handle GLOC's 20+ appointment types per surgeon
Org just 3x'd in size via two acquisitions — internal chaos could slow implementation cooperation
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Kennedy to close Maya DID/phone routing requirements; Carli to close Athena 3rd-party auth. Brantley to create Monarch Drive folder + design partner onboarding SOP (per Kapeesh). Formal kickoff call post May 16. Owner: Brantley + implementation team.

per_deal_summaries.status: In final legal/contracting. As of May 11, deal described as 'closing this week' with a 6-month structure. Legal has been the chronic bottleneck — Banner's 90-day cycle runs three approval stages: legal review → architectural review (2–3 months) → implementation. April brought a direct legal meeting (Amanda as advocate) to break through the redline deadlock, focusing on liability coverage, SLA alignment, and service modification visibility. Todd Werner requiring a 'term for convenience' clause.
key_risks: 90-day legal cycle has historically slipped — architectural review still ahead after legal approval
Todd Werner's 'term for convenience' clause requires internal sign-off at Solv
Epic implementation complexity adds months post-contract close
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Close 6-month contract — Owner: Teresa/Brantley — Due: Week of May 11 (may be executing now)

per_deal_summaries.size: $3,500/mo up to 3K telemed visits + $2K implementation + $0.50/ClearPay + 1% Solv Pay; 6-month initial term
per_deal_summaries.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR ops/technical lead); Dr. Attia (clinical); Dr. Ammar Baba (decision-maker, engaged but skeptical)
per_deal_summaries.status: Post-demo (May 5) — deal regressed from near-paper-process back to Clear MSP. Dr. Baba attended the May 5 full telehealth demo and is interested but guarded; has been burned by integrations before and explicitly said 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know' — ECW proof is now the explicit gate. Two deal tracks: UC Telemed (priority) and Specialties as a design partner target (Kapeesh call pending). CLS feedback compiled into a doc for Maria Kwok (May 6). CLS pushing on pricing: per-encounter model or hybrid, 3–6 months free/discounted ramp, 6-month first term, waived $2K implementation fee. President of CLS engaged; sees this potentially replacing Luma for all primary care scheduling at 300+ providers.
key_risks: Dr. Baba burned by ECW integrations before — ECW proof video and a Texas/multi-provider reference are the explicit gate items
HMO/PCP billing logic unresolved — place-of-service 20 workaround unconfirmed; many CLS patients have assigned PCPs
Pricing concession requests (flat fee → per-encounter, shorter term) could stall contract or set a bad precedent
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley/Danny to deliver ECW integration demo video (HIPAA-blurred) and find ECW/multi-provider/Texas reference customer — due ASAP (1–2 days post May 5). Kapeesh to schedule specialty design partner scope call. Owner: Brantley/Danny.

per_deal_summaries.size: 20 providers, 10-person contact center (expanding to 16), $200K+ annual staffing cost; largest GI practice in New Mexico
per_deal_summaries.ehr: ModMed (gGastro) — migrating from Athena, EMR go-live June 16 (may slip)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Albert Lujan (Director, IT & Infrastructure) — controls vendor recommendations to board
per_deal_summaries.status: Strong May 11 discovery call. Buyer intent is very high — shareholders pushing for AI scheduling, every prior vendor has failed on gGastro integration, Albert frustrated no one has solved it. Direct quote: 'If you can show a working proof of concept, it's almost a done deal.' Core blocker: gGastro has no write-back API (per Jenn's prior research). Kapeesh confirmed May 12 that gGastro is a distinct ModMed product and Solv needs to decide if they want to build to it. Brantley ran market sizing: ~70–82% of independent GI practices in ICP are on gGastro (~500 practices, 5–30 providers). Jenn scoping ModMed synapSYS Marketplace — recommends $7,500 Elite tier for UI sandbox. Albert is meeting with a Chicago GI group this week who may have a competitor with working ModMed AI scheduling.
key_risks: gGastro write-back API gap is unproven — every competitor Albert talked to hit this wall
Chicago GI competitor call this week could surface a working rival before Solv confirms feasibility
June 16 ModMed go-live creates a tight integration window; missing it likely delays the deal by months
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to confirm gGastro integration feasibility with Kapeesh/Jenn/Carli — follow up with Albert within 1–2 days (window from May 11 may already be passed). Owner: Brantley + Kapeesh.

per_deal_summaries.size: $800/mo per location + $1K one-time setup; 3 existing locs + new standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1
per_deal_summaries.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user); Christopher LaFave (Executive Director)
per_deal_summaries.status: Strong April 8 demo with full leadership team (Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). Team engaged, confirmed verbal interest, asked operational questions, validated urgent care use case as a clean fit. Internal review done; team committed to reconnect in ~1 week. ECW integration proof is the hard gate — Christopher explicitly pushed to see data actually flowing into ECW. June 1 urgent care opening is a real forcing function. Note: a Granola pipeline summary separately flagged this as 'Passed — ECW build not justified for single UC location'; that may reflect an internal Solv discussion, but Slack shows active engagement through April 12.
key_risks: ECW integration confidence is a hard gate — Christopher needs to see live data flow, not just hear about it
HMO referral requirement unresolved for specialty (non-urgent) clinics — blocks expansion to 3 other ortho locations
Possible Solv internal decision to deprioritize (per Granola pipeline flag) — needs clarification
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to clarify internal Solv disposition — active or passed? If active, send ECW integration documentation packet (was due post-April 12; likely overdue). Escalate HMO referral workflow gap to Kapeesh. Owner: Brantley.

per_deal_summaries.status: Formally paused since March 11. Jon proactively communicated (did not ghost) that Assort Health's contractual obligations block all movement until their vendor exit resolves — no timeline given. Deal was near-signature in late February before Assort surfaced. Jon confirmed intent to return; said they are 'very interested.' Last active check-in was April 7 — 5+ weeks since last touch.
key_risks: Assort exit timeline is entirely outside Solv's control — could be weeks or months
5+ weeks since last touch — Jon's internal advocacy may erode; check-in cadence has slipped
Competitor could step in during the open window
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to check in with Jon on Assort exit status — overdue, should happen this week. GLOC provides Athena ortho reference context useful when deal resumes. Owner: Brantley.

per_deal_summaries.size: 4 locations (3 new, 1 struggling Atlanta/Kennesaw location); cash constrained
per_deal_summaries.champion: Randy Johansen (board member, former AFC CEO) — board-level, not operational
per_deal_summaries.status: Randy connected at UCA April 20 (intro by Heather). April 24 call: OS1 opened 3 locations simultaneously and is cash-constrained; Atlanta location opened for months without an MRI and actively hurting revenue. Randy agreed to share ClearPay + Maya materials before an internal OS1 meeting (~April 29). No update since April 24; status unknown as of May 14.
key_risks: Cash constraints are real — opening 3 locations at once stressed the org and limits near-term spend
Randy is a board member, not an operator — no confirmed operational champion driving internally
No contact or Slack update in ~3 weeks
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Brantley to follow up with Randy; confirm if internal OS1 meeting happened and identify operational contact. Owner: Brantley — overdue, check in now.

per_deal_summaries.status: Cut off April 7. TPC won't replace their CTO-built scheduler ('too many resources poured into it'). Rikin proposed Solv as an acquisition-only layer alongside their scheduler — non-standard scope, not on Solv's near-term roadmap. Also piloting a competing voice agent. 6–9 month horizon before this could realistically re-engage.
key_risks: Non-standard deal structure (acquisition-only) not on Solv roadmap
6–9 month horizon with a competing voice pilot already in progress
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Closed. Revisit Q4 2026 if acquisition-layer deal model gains leadership traction internally.

per_deal_summaries.champion: Osvaldo Ramirez (Administrator) — not the decision-maker; providers hold final authority
per_deal_summaries.status: Closed out April 7. No response since the February 18 intro call despite 6+ weeks of follow-ups and consistent email opens. Providers (real decision-makers) were never directly engaged. Prior vendor burned them on hidden fees — trust was a prerequisite never fully established.
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Closed. No further action. Revisit only if Osvaldo re-engages post-Athena migration.

per_deal_summaries.status: Deal broken off by Solv late March. No Dr. Chrono integration for ClearPay. Per-location pricing model didn't fit their 2-provider/6-location setup. Dr. Chrono EHR flagged by Kapeesh as a blocker.

per_deal_summaries.deal_type: Expansion (existing Solv partner via Mederi Urgent Care)
per_deal_summaries.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator); Dr. Tovmassian (owner, also owns Mederi Urgent Care)
per_deal_summaries.status: ClearPay only — not a Monarch deal. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back March 16: 'Doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Point-of-care estimate workflow doesn't fit primary care with stable recurring patient mix. No response to follow-ups since. Not actively pursuing to preserve the Mederi partner relationship.
key_risks: Real collection pain exists but ClearPay product-market fit unclear for primary care recurring patient mix
per_deal_summaries.next_step: Not actively pursuing. One final gentle follow-up given partner relationship. No Monarch angle here.

leadership_table.key_notes: Signed Apr 27; Maya + ClearPay kickoff week of May 17.
Reportedly closing now (6-mo structure); 90-day legal cycle has been the chronic blocker.
ECW proof video + Texas reference urgently needed; deal gated on integration credibility.
'Basically done' if gGastro integration shown; competitor risk surfacing this week.
ECW live data demo is hard gate; June 1 urgent care opening is forcing function. Internal disposition unclear.
Assort Health contract blocking since Mar 11; Jon check-in 5+ weeks overdue.
No update from board contact Randy since Apr 24; cash constraints limit near-term spend.
